steps.title: Set up the equation using exponent rules
steps.content:
Write the function values: $$f(8)=c^8$$ and $$f(6)=c^6$$.
Substitute into the given condition $$f(8)=16\cdot f(6)$$: $$c^8 = 16 \cdot c^6$$.
Since $$c\gt 0$$, divide both sides by $$c^6$$: $$c^2 = 16$$.

steps.title: Solve for c and select the answer
steps.content:
Take the square root of both sides: $$c = \sqrt{16} = 4$$ (the negative root is excluded because $$c\gt 1$$).
The answer is 4.
